title: Luisia tristis
taxonomy: Luisia tristis is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Luisia and family Orchidaceae. Within the Orchidaceae family, it is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. The subfamily Orchidoideae is a large and diverse group that includes many species of terrestrial orchids. Luisia tristis is native to tropical regions in Asia, where it grows in shaded areas and rocky outcrops.

intro: Luisia tristis, also known as the Pale Luisia, is a species of terrestrial orchid native to tropical Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Luisia tristis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Luisia tristis is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
